attribution: Contains material derived from Wikipedia, licensed CC BY-SA 4.0: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Vietnamese_dishes and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Vietnamese_culinary_specialities

entries.name: Bánh canh
entries.desc: Thick noodle [ 1 ] [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh đa cua
entries.desc: Rice paper with crab broth

entries.name: Bánh hỏi
entries.desc: Extremely thin noodles that are woven into intricate bundles and often topped with chopped scallions (spring onions) and a complementary meat dish [ 1 ]

entries.name: Bánh tằm cà ri
entries.desc: Spicy chicken curry over special rice noodles

entries.name: Bún bò Huế
entries.desc: Huế style (hot and spicy) beef rice vermicelli soup with lemongrass and pork [ 3 ] [ 4 ]

entries.name: Bún bung
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli cooked with pork ribs and "dọc mùng"

entries.name: Bún cá
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli and grilled fish balls mixed with fish sauce and raw vegetables

entries.name: Bún chả cá
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli soup with fried fishcake

entries.name: Chạo tôm
entries.desc: Barbecued shrimp paste/mousse on a sugar cane stick

entries.name: Bún chả
entries.desc: Vermicelli noodles with grilled pork meatballs served over salad, herbs, bean sprouts, and sliced cucumbers

entries.name: Bún đậu mắm tôm
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli with fried beans and shrimp paste

entries.name: Bún mắm
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli with broth made from fish sauce or choke fish

entries.name: Bún mọc
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli with sprouted broth

entries.name: Bún măng vịt
entries.desc: Bamboo shoots and duck noodle soup. [ 3 ]

entries.name: Bún ốc
entries.desc: Noodles with snails

entries.name: Bún riêu
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli in a tomato and crab broth [ 3 ] [ 5 ]

entries.name: Bún thịt nướng
entries.desc: Grilled pork (often shredded) and cold vermicelli noodles over a bed of greens (salad and sliced cucumber), herbs and bean sprouts. Also often includes a few chopped up egg rolls, spring onions, and shrimp. Served with roasted peanuts on top and a small bowl of nước chấm . [ 1 ] [ 3 ] [ 6 ]

entries.name: Bún thang
entries.desc: Rice vermicelli served with broth and various other ingredients – laksa leaves, Chinese coriander, thin fried chicken eggs, shredded chicken breast, and thinly sliced pork rolls

entries.name: Cao lầu
entries.desc: Originated from Quang Nam Province in central Vietnam, mì quảng is adamant about maintaining a variety of colors and textures, featuring a pork and shrimp-based broth, the chewy yellow of the thick turmeric noodles, the bright green ribbons of lettuce, the soft earthy pork, the tenderness of the shrimp, the pleasant crunch of crushed peanut between your teeth, and the snap of the sesame-freckled rice crackers.

entries.name: Cellophane noodles miến , bún tàu , bún tào
entries.desc: Transparent noodle made from starch and water, generally sold in dried form, soaked to reconstitute, then used in soups, stir-fried dishes, or spring rolls.

entries.name: Hủ tiếu
entries.desc: Chinese/Cambodian noodle soup traditionally consisting of rice noodles with pork broth.

entries.name: Mì or Súp mì
entries.desc: Vietnamese/Chinese noodle soup with yellow wheat noodles brought over by Chinese immigrants.

entries.name: Mì Quảng
entries.desc: Ingredients often vary, but dishes most often consist of wide rice noodles served with little broth, pork chops, chicken, shrimp, vegetables, peanuts, and bánh tráng .

entries.name: Mì xào giòn
entries.desc: Deep-fried egg noodles, topped with seafood, vegetables, and a sauce

entries.name: Phở
entries.desc: Noodle soup served with various cuts of beef and onions. [ 5 ] Often eaten with basil, mint, lime, and bean sprouts

entries.name: Miến lươn
entries.desc: Northern Vietnam , North Central Coast ( Nghệ An )

entries.name: Miến trộn
entries.desc: Stir-fried rice vermicelli mixed with shrimp or crab, seasoned with sweet and spicy spices

entries.name: Rice noodles bánh canh , bánh phở
entries.desc: Made from rice flour

entries.name: Rice vermicelli bún
entries.desc: A type of rice noodles

entries.name: Bánh ú
entries.desc: Bak Chang dumplings – like Chinese zongzi

entries.name: Bánh bao
entries.desc: Ball-shaped bun filled with pork and other ingredients [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh bột lọc
entries.desc: Wrapper made of tapioca starch packed with shrimp [ 2 ] [ 4 ]

entries.name: Bánh bột lọc trần
entries.desc: Dumplings with wrappers made of tapioca starch; similar to chaozhou fun guo

entries.name: Bánh bột lọc lá
entries.desc: Tiny rice flour dumplings stuffed with shrimp and ground pork and wrapped in a banana leaf; from Huế

entries.name: Bánh cam
entries.desc: Deep-fried glutinous rice sesame balls filled with sweetened mung bean paste; from southern Vietnam

entries.name: Bánh ít
entries.desc: Small stuffed glutinous rice flour balls [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh ít trần
entries.desc: "Naked" small stuffed glutinous rice flour balls

entries.name: Bánh khúc
entries.desc: Glutinous rice ball filled with mung bean and fatty pork

entries.name: Bánh nậm
entries.desc: Flat rice flour dumpling from Huế stuffed with minced pork and mushroom, and seasoned with pepper and spices; wrapped in a banana leaf [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh phu thê
entries.desc: Literally "husband and wife cake"; a sweet cake made of rice or tapioca flour and gelatin, filled with mung bean paste; also spelled bánh xu xê (su sê)

entries.name: Bánh phu thê bột bán
entries.desc: Husband and wife cakes made with tapioca pearls

entries.name: Bánh rán
entries.desc: Deep-fried glutinous rice sesame balls filled with sweetened mung bean paste; from northern Vietnam [ 2 ] [ 7 ]

entries.name: Bánh tẻ
entries.desc: Small steamed rice cake wrapped with leaves of some local trees into a long, thin cylindrical shape, and boiled thoroughly.

entries.name: Bánh giò
entries.desc: Made from filtered flour, lean shoulder meat, wood ear, dried onion, pepper, fish sauce, salt... Bánh giò has a long, jutting shape like an upside down hand.

entries.name: Bánh bèo
entries.desc: Small steamed savory rice cakes [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh bột lọc
entries.desc: Rice dumplings made in a clear rice flour batter stuffed with shrimp and ground pork. Cooked inside a banana leaf.

entries.name: Bánh căn
entries.desc: A southern specialty consisting of small pancakes made with quail eggs, cooked in small clay pans [ 1 ]

entries.name: Bánh đúc
entries.desc: Rice cake or corn cake eaten as a dessert or savory meal

entries.name: Bánh khọt
entries.desc: A southern specialty consisting of small, fried rice flour pancakes [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh mì
entries.desc: Vietnamese sandwich [ 8 ]

entries.name: Bánh rế
entries.desc: Bình Thuận Province

entries.name: Bánh xèo
entries.desc: Coconut milk-flavored crepes [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 9 ]

entries.name: Bánh bột chiên
entries.desc: Fried rice cake with eggs and tangy soy sauce.

entries.name: Bánh phồng tôm
entries.desc: A form of deep fried snack made from starch and prawn.

entries.name: Bánh cống
entries.desc: Minced pork mixed with tapioca root and whole green beans, served with a circled shrimp on top.

entries.name: Bánh quai vạc
entries.desc: Made from tapioca starch, filled with shrimp and meat inside.

entries.name: Bánh tai
entries.desc: Made from plain rice flour, pork and some other spices.

entries.name: Bánh tai heo
entries.desc: Made from flour, granulated sugar, white sesame, condensed milk, eggs, butter, vanilla, cooking oil, salt, water... and processed by deep frying.

entries.name: Bánh cuốn
entries.desc: Steamed rice roll [ 2 ] [ 10 ]

entries.name: Bánh tôm
entries.desc: Shrimp patties [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh tôm Hồ Tây
entries.desc: A shrimp patty specialty originating from the area around West Lake ( Tây Hồ ), Hanoi

entries.name: Bánh tráng
entries.desc: Central Vietnam, Binh Dinh

entries.name: Bánh ướt
entries.desc: Steamed rice paper [ 2 ]

entries.name: Chả giò or Nem rán
entries.desc: Fried pork spring rolls

entries.name: Gỏi cuốn
entries.desc: Soft vermicelli summer roll

entries.name: Gỏi cá
entries.desc: Raw fish meat wrapped in rice paper rolls with herbs, served with dipping sauce

entries.name: Gỏi cá trích
entries.desc: Raw herring salad

entries.name: Món cuốn
entries.desc: Roll which includes a variety of ingredients rolled in rice paper (bánh tráng)

entries.name: Popiah bò bía
entries.desc: Spring roll wrapped in a soft, thin paper-like crepe or pancake made from wheat flour

entries.name: Trảng Bàng dew-wetted rice paper bánh tráng phơi sương Trảng Bàng
entries.desc: Tây Ninh Province

entries.name: Cơm nắm
entries.desc: Northern Vietnam

entries.name: Cơm tấm
entries.desc: Warm broken rice often served with a slab of grilled pork chop marinated in sugar and fish sauce (sườn), a slice of steamed pork loaf topped with egg yolks ( chả trứng hấp), and a mixture of pork skin and thinly shredded pork (bì).

entries.name: Cơm rượu
entries.desc: Dessert from Southern Vietnam, made from glutinous rice

entries.name: Cơm cháy
entries.desc: Crispy golden brown rice at the bottom of the cooking pot – which is then left dried in natural sunlight for 2–3 days

entries.name: Cơm hến
entries.desc: A rice dish made from cooked baby river mussels (basket clams), rice, peanuts, pork rinds, shrimp paste, chili paste, starfruits and bạc hà stems. It is normally served with the broth of cooked mussels

entries.name: Cơm gà Quảng Nam
entries.desc: Cooked rice served with boiled chicken and sprinkled with chicken broth

entries.name: Cơm lam
entries.desc: Glutinous rice and other ingredients placed in bamboo tubes and cooked on fire

entries.name: Cơm chiên
entries.desc: Rice fried with meat, eggs, and vegetables

entries.name: Xôi
entries.desc: Sweet (ngọt) or savory (mặn) dish made from glutinous rice and other ingredients.

entries.name: Oản
entries.desc: White sticky rice, granulated sugar or cake flour formed into a truncated pyramid shape – used as an offering in Buddhist temples.

entries.name: Xôi chiên
entries.desc: White sticky rice flattened and deep fried in oil.

entries.name: Xôi đỗ xanh
entries.desc: Mung beans are scrubbed, soaked and peeled – then mixed with glutinous rice and steamed.

entries.name: Xôi gà
entries.desc: White sticky rice cooked with coconut milk and shredded boiled/ roasted chicken, then served in bowls or pandan leaves.

entries.name: Xôi gấc
entries.desc: Sticky rice cooked with gac meat to create a bright red color and delicious flavor.

entries.name: Xôi lá cẩm
entries.desc: Sticky rice cooked in Camellia leaves to create a red purple color.

entries.name: Xôi đậu phộng
entries.desc: Soaked glutinous rice cooked with boiled peanuts.

entries.name: Xôi ngũ sắc
entries.desc: Sticky rice cooked with multiple-colored rice leaves to create 5 colors that represent the Five Elements

entries.name: Xôi sắn
entries.desc: Glutinous rice mixed with glutinous cassava roots that have been grated or cut into small pieces, then cooked and seasoned with fried onions and oil.

entries.name: Xôi vò
entries.desc: Sticky rice cooked separately with pandan leaves for aroma, and turmeric powder for color.

entries.name: Xôi xéo
entries.desc: Sticky rice placed into a bowl, then cut into slices with a knife, and sprinkled with green beans and fried onions.

entries.name: Bird's nest soup súp yến sào
entries.desc: Bird's nest soup

entries.name: Canh chua
entries.desc: Hot and sour soup

entries.name: Canh khoai mỡ
entries.desc: Purple yam soup

entries.name: Khổ qua nhồi thịt
entries.desc: Bitter gourd stuffed with meat, then bundled with scallions and cooked in bone broth

entries.name: Ốc nấu chuối đậu
entries.desc: A rustic but quite sophisticated dish that consists of boiled snails cooked with green bananas, fried beans, bacon, adorned with guise leaves and perilla

entries.name: Congee cháo
entries.desc: Rice porridge

entries.name: Cháo lòng
entries.desc: Rice porridge cooked in broth made from pork bones and boiled pork intestines

entries.name: Lẩu
entries.desc: Spicy sour soup with assorted vegetables, meats, and seafood

entries.name: Phá lấu
entries.desc: Pork offal stew

entries.name: Súp măng cua
entries.desc: Asparagus-crab combination soup

entries.name: Thịt kho tàu
entries.desc: Fatty pork and eggs braised in coconut juice

entries.name: Bò kho
entries.desc: Beef and vegetable stew [ 1 ]

entries.name: Balut trứng vịt lộn or hột vịt lộn
entries.desc: Fertilized duck (or chicken) egg with a nearly-developed embryo inside that is boiled and eaten in the shell

entries.name: Bánh
entries.desc: "Cake" or "bread"

entries.name: Bánh chưng
entries.desc: Square-shaped steamed glutinous rice dumpling wrapped in a Phrynium placentarium leaf ( lá dong ) [ 2 ] The dish is a traditionally served around Vietnamese New Year to be eaten with pickled leeks

entries.name: Bánh giầy
entries.desc: White, flat, and round glutinous rice cake, wrapped in cut pieces of banana leaves, and usually served with a type of Vietnamese sausage chả lụa

entries.name: Bánh đa nướng
entries.desc: Called Bánh tráng nướng in the South, large round flat rice crackers

entries.name: Bánh gối
entries.desc: Deep fried pastry stuffed with cellophane noodles , minced pork, cloud ear fungus and thin slices of chinese sausage [ 7 ]

entries.name: Bánh lá
entries.desc: Parcel of a variety of rice stuffed with some fillings and wrapped in a leaf or leaves.

entries.name: Black pudding dồi tiết' (Northern) or dồi huyết (Southern)
entries.desc: Blood sausage

entries.name: Bò 7 món
entries.desc: 7 courses of beef

entries.name: Chả
entries.desc: Sausage

entries.name: Cốm
entries.desc: Young rice, roasted and wrapped in lotus leaves; the accompaniment is ripened bananas

entries.name: Giò lụa
entries.desc: A pork sausage, similar to Bologna sausage

entries.name: Lò trấu
entries.desc: Fixed Rice Husk Stove

entries.name: Nem chua
entries.desc: Cured fermented pork

entries.name: Ô mai
entries.desc: Snack made from salted dried apricot or sugared dried apricot

entries.name: Ốc bươu nhồi thịt
entries.desc: Minced snail mixed with ground pork, carrot, black mushroom stuffed in snail shell

entries.name: Rousong chà bông
entries.desc: Meat floss

entries.name: Bò nướng lá lốt
entries.desc: Ground beef wrapped in lolot (NEVER grape leaves)

entries.name: Tiết canh
entries.desc: Fresh animal blood mixed with fish sauce/ light salted water – used with chopped meat and animal cartilage

entries.name: You tiao quẩy
entries.desc: A long, golden-brown, deep fried strip of dough

entries.name: Bánh bò
entries.desc: "Cow cake" (literal name in Vietnamese), made from glutinous rice flour and coconut milk, with a honeycomb-like texture [ 2 ]

entries.name: Bánh rế
entries.desc: Bánh rế is a Vietnamese street food made from sweet potatoes. The sweet potato is made into a pancake, deep-fried, then sugared.

entries.name: Bánh cáy
entries.desc: Rectangular-shaped sweet dessert made by roasting and grinding glutinous rice and other ingredients

entries.name: Bánh da lợn
entries.desc: Colored steamed layer cake made from tapioca starch, rice flour, coconut milk, water, sugar, and other ingredients

entries.name: Bánh chuối
entries.desc: Banana cake [ 7 ]

entries.name: Bánh cốm
entries.desc: Made from cốm , green bean paste, grated coconut and pumpkin jam or naked lotus jam. Often used for engagement ceremonies

entries.name: Bánh đậu xanh
entries.desc: Made from ground mung bean paste with sugar and vegetable oil or animal fat

entries.name: Bánh gai
entries.desc: The crust is made up of glutinous rice flour and hemp leaves; the filling is of finely chopped lard, banana essential oil, coconut rice, and cooked green beans

entries.name: Bánh tro
entries.desc: Leaves are burned into ashes and then soaked with glutinous rice to create a golden brown color like amber. Served with sugar or honey/ molasses.

entries.name: Bánh in
entries.desc: A Vietnamese cake from the Huế, often sold in small rectangular shaped snack packs. The main ingredients include mung bean, rice flour and durian

entries.name: Bánh Flan
entries.desc: Vietnamese adaptation of flan that was introduced during French colonization. Often used condensed milk as the base for a somewhat denser and heavier texture. [ 11 ]

entries.name: Bánh kẹp lá dứa
entries.desc: Pandan and coconut flavored waffle eaten plain

entries.name: Bánh khoai mì
entries.desc: Sweet cassava cake

entries.name: Bánh khảo
entries.desc: Glutinous flour roasted with sugar, filled with pumpkin jam and fat

entries.name: Bánh lọt
entries.desc: Made from rice flour and tapioca starch with the aroma of coconut milk, sugar, and the green and white color of pandan leaves

entries.name: Bánh mật
entries.desc: Made from glutinous rice flour mixed with mung bean molasses, and wrapped in banana leaves

entries.name: Bánh nhãn
entries.desc: Made from fragrant glutinous rice or Hai Hau yellow sticky rice, chicken eggs, white sugar and lard

entries.name: Bánh pía
entries.desc: Teochew-style with durian

entries.name: Bánh tét
entries.desc: Log-shaped cylindrical glutinous rice cake, wrapped in a banana leaf and filled with a meat or vegetarian filling

entries.name: Bánh tiêu
entries.desc: Hollow doughnuts

entries.name: Bánh trung thu
entries.desc: Mooncake [ 2 ]

entries.name: Chè
entries.desc: Sweet dessert soup or pudding [ 1 ] [ 12 ]

entries.name: Chè trôi nước
entries.desc: Dessert consisting of balls made from mung bean paste wrapped in a shell made of glutinous rice flour.

entries.name: Chè bánh lọt
entries.desc: Coconut milk, a worm-like jelly made from rice flour with green food coloring (usually derived from the pandan leaf), shaved ice and palm sugar

entries.name: Chuối chiên
entries.desc: Banana fried in a batter

entries.name: Coconut candy kẹo dừa
entries.desc: Candy made with coconut milk and coconut cream

entries.name: Sữa chua
entries.desc: Yogurt made with condensed milk

entries.name: Douhua tào phớ
entries.desc: Soybean pudding

entries.name: Grass jelly thạch sương sáo
entries.desc: Made by boiling the aged and slightly oxidized stalks and leaves of Mesona chinensis (member of the mint family) with potassium carbonate for several hours with a little starch and then cooling the liquid to a jelly-like consistency

entries.name: Mè xửng Huế
entries.desc: A chewy candy originally from the city of Huế, made with boiled down cane sugar, (into a soft and thick solid), coated with sesame seeds, and usually containing peanuts.

entries.name: Bánh trôi
entries.desc: Boiled Glutinous rice balls – also called bánh chay; literally "floating cake") – served together with bánh chay [ 12 ]

entries.name: Tapioca pudding chè chuối
entries.desc: Made from bananas and tapioca

entries.name: Taro cake bánh khoai môn
entries.desc: Taro cake [ 7 ]

entries.name: Thạch rau câu or sương sa
entries.desc: Gelatin dessert

entries.name: Rượu nếp
entries.desc: Sweet dessert made from black glutinous rice.

entries.name: Sâm bổ lượng
entries.desc: Sweet, cold soup

entries.name: Chẳm chéo
entries.desc: Based on Thai sauce, nam chim chaeo (or nam jim jaew) brought over by ethnic Thai people in Vietnam. Main ingredients are coriander, bird's eye chili , garlic, different herbs, hạt dổi and mắc khén (Indian prickly ash; a variety of Sichuan pepper ). There are different types of chẳm chéo as well. Eaten with vegetables, meats, and sometimes fruit.

entries.name: Fish sauce nước mắm
entries.desc: A base condiment that is derived from fish that have been allowed to ferment. It is normally used as seasoning during cooking or a base for sauces with other ingredients and is not to be used by itself.

entries.name: Hoisin sauce tương đen
entries.desc: A popular condiment for phở in Ho Chi Minh City

entries.name: Muối ớt rau răm
entries.desc: Dipping sauce made with Vietnamese coriander , bird's eye chilis , lime, and other ingredients. Used as a dipping sauce for meats or trứng vịt lộn .

entries.name: Muối ớt xanh
entries.desc: Dipping sauce with green bird's eye chilis and kaffir lime leaves as main ingredients. Usually served with seafood.

entries.name: Nước chấm
entries.desc: Dipping sauce with fish sauce, water, sugar, lime and sometimes chilis and ginger.

entries.name: Peanut sauce tương đậu phộng
entries.desc: Used in cuốn diếp

entries.name: Sa tế
entries.desc: Chợ Lớn, Ho Chi Minh City , Southern Vietnam

entries.name: Shrimp paste mắm tôm
entries.desc: Shrimp paste

entries.name: Soy sauce xì dầu or nước tương
entries.desc: Condiment produced from fermented soybeans

entries.name: Tương
entries.desc: Condiments made from fermented bean paste

entries.name: Tương ớt
entries.desc: Hot/chili sauce that varies in different regions.

entries.name: Bia hơi
entries.desc: Fresh beer

entries.name: Chanh muối
entries.desc: Salty limeade [ 12 ]

entries.name: Nước mía
entries.desc: Sugar cane juice often mixed with kumquat juice [ 13 ]

entries.name: Nước sâm
entries.desc: Iced ginseng drink

entries.name: Nước sắn dây hoa bưởi
entries.desc: Made of kudzu and pomelo flower extract.

entries.name: Rau má
entries.desc: Iced pennywort juice often sweetened

entries.name: Rượu cần
entries.desc: Tây Nguyên and Tây Bắc

entries.name: Rượu đế
entries.desc: Mekong Delta

entries.name: Rượu thuốc
entries.desc: Medicinal liquor

entries.name: Snake wine rượu rắn
entries.desc: An alcoholic beverage produced by infusing whole snakes in rice wine or grain alcohol

entries.name: Egg soda soda sữa hột gà
entries.desc: Iced soda with egg, sugar and milk

entries.name: Soy milk sữa đậu nành
entries.desc: Beverage made from soybeans

entries.name: Vietnamese iced coffee cà phê sữa đá
entries.desc: Traditional Vietnamese coffee, made using medium to coarse ground dark roast Vietnamese-grown coffee with a small metal Vietnamese drip filter (phin cà phê)

entries.name: Vietnamese lotus tea trà sen , chè sen , or chè ướp sen
entries.desc: A type of green tea produced in Vietnam that has been flavored with the scent of Nelumbo nucifera

entries.name: Vietnamese tea trà Việt
entries.desc: Green tea , similar to Keemun and Yunnan , but more sweet

entries.name: Vietnamese wine
entries.desc: Wine produced in Vietnam
